steps.title: Identify what intermittency requires in the data
steps.content: “Intermittent” wastewater means influxes arrive in episodes, not continuously. If they were constant, delta 15-N values would remain elevated throughout. Evidence of intermittency must show values fluctuating—rising when wastewater arrives, falling when it doesn't.

steps.title: Match this pattern to the choices
steps.content:
The correct answer shows Akumal Bay and Tulum fluctuated considerably across three measurements: Akumal (3.3 → 2.0 → 6.3) and Tulum (5.9 → 2.3 → 5.5).

This rising-falling-rising pattern reflects wastewater that comes and goes, supporting the intermittent conclusion.

The other choices don't demonstrate this fluctuation:
  • A presents an exception rather than the overall pattern
  • B focuses on extreme values without showing how they vary over time
  • C claims relative stability


That is the correct answer.
